title: Food insecurity hotspots are emerging due to conflict, climate change, and economic shocks
subtitle: Drivers of food insecurity hotspots by country
textParagraph: The primary drivers of food insecurity in these countries are often linked to conflict, such as insecurity, displacement, and social instability, as well as climate change factors, including droughts, flooding, and tropical cyclones. Additional contributing factors include the economic impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic, which has left many countries struggling to recover from significant job losses.[reference: wdr22] Additionally, rising global food prices have hit hardest in areas reliant on imports due to their lack of local food production [reference: unctad-trade].
visdescription: The primary drivers of food insecurity in these countries are often linked to conflict, such as insecurity, displacement, and social instability, as well as climate change factors, including droughts, flooding, and tropical cyclones.
